item.title: SA Liberal candidate no longer running for party after podcast comments
item.description: SA Liberal Leader Ashton Hurn says one of her candidates for the upcoming state election is no longer running for the party after comments he made on Islam, feminism, same-sex marriage and "the trans agenda" came to light from an American podcast.

item.title: Oil price breaks the $US100 per barrel mark as oil tankers are hit in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The oil price has surged again amid Iranian strikes on oil tankers and the fear there could be more attacks to come.

item.title: Fuel standards relaxed to add 100 million litres of fuel to Australian supply
item.description: Australia will bring in 100 million more litres of petrol by allowing a higher level of sulfur content in its fuel for the next 60 days, Energy Minister Chris Bowen says, as the country reckons with petrol flows choked by the Middle East war.

item.title: Tigers hopeful Heamasi Makasini stays with NRL club amid talk of rugby union switch
item.description: Heamasi Makasini has shown off his talent in both rugby codes, but Wests Tigers are hopeful they will keep the 18-year-old at the club once his contract expires.

item.title: Wife of Australian footballer Barry Cable takes stand in his child sex abuse trial
item.description: The wife of Barry Cable, the former footballer on trial for allegedly sexually abusing a child in the early stages of his illustrious career, testifies in defence of her famous husband.

item.title: Albanese government won't say the 'w' word about Iran
item.description: This week in Canberra the Nationals chose a new leader, the energy minister chose his words carefully and the government chose to avoid one word altogether.

item.title: Novak Djokovic loses to Jack Draper in three sets at Indian Wells tournament
item.description: World number three Novak Djokovic falls to defending champion Jack Draper in a pulsating fourth-round match at the prestigious Indian Wells tournament.

item.title: Disaster assistance payments have been expanded in the Northern Territory. Here's what you need to know
item.description: Residents in more flood-affected communities across the Top End are now eligible for hardship payments, with disaster assistance also extended further in the Barkly and Central Australia.

item.title: Aftermath of drone strike targeting a car in Beirut
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Roosters NRL player Billy Smith involved in e-bike accident ahead of Rabbitohs clash
item.description: Sydney Roosters centre Billy Smith will miss Friday night's match against the Rabbitohs after he was involved in an e-bike accident near the club's headquarters.

item.title: 'Black rain' in Tehran following Israeli strikes on oil depots
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Man who killed Perth music promoter Andrew Scally in suburban street sentenced to jail
item.description: Jardyn Rigo is sentenced to more than six years in jail for killing music promoter Andrew Scally in a street fight.

item.title: Parents and disability advocates shocked as specialist service AEIOU closes with no notice
item.description: In a statement published on Wednesday, early intervention care provider AEIOU announced the board had appointed external administrators to the organisation.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Raso, Catley ruled out, with Matildas prepared for anything against North Korea
item.description: Hayley Raso and Steph Catley will miss the Women's Asian Cup quarterfinal, as the team prepares for the unknown quantities that North Korea may bring.

item.title: Blues hang on for scratchy but vital win over Tigers at the MCG
item.description: Carlton kicks only one goal in the second half, but it is just enough to hold off a late charge by a wayward Richmond at the MCG.

item.title: Hobart Clinic chief resigns months after appointment amid reported clash with board
item.description: The Hobart Clinic's chief executive has resigned as the state government commissions a review of the mental health hospital's corporate governance.

item.title: Thursday Finance with David Chau
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: 'Absolute madness': Broncos remain winless after Eels win bizarre NRL clash
item.description: The Broncos' defence is labelled "pathetic" as they surrender a 20-6 lead, hit back in the second half but eventually lose 40-32 on the back of future teammate Jonah Pezet's game-sealing try.

item.title: Drought-hit WA pastoralists plead for funds to move hay stored 1,000km away
item.description: After years of drought, pastoralists in WA are desperate for fodder for their cattle. A charity has a supply in storage for them — but no money for fuel to transport it.

item.title: Kurdish fighters ready to take back Iran
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Former Israeli Defence Minister on the Iranian conflict
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Firefighters battle burning fuel tanks in video released by Bahrain government
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Oil tankers burn as Iran's new leader vows to keep Strait of Hormuz closed
item.description: Iran's new supreme leader says the Strait of Hormuz should remain closed as a tool of pressure, vowing to "obtain compensation from the enemy".

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: What time do the Matildas play? How to watch and follow Australia vs North Korea
item.description: The knockout stage of the Women's Asian Cup is here, and tonight the Matildas will face the might of North Korea in their quarter-final in Perth.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: The full-throttle, mysterious North Korean side looking to rain on the Matildas' parade
item.description: Sensational protests, head-scratching substitutions and some seriously impressive play — North Korea is one of the Asian Cup's best sides, and certainly its most mysterious. Here is what the Matildas can expect in their blockbuster quarterfinal tonight in Perth.

item.title: Hugo Weaving talks about loving art, hating learning lines, and what's next
item.description: Hugo Weaving may be an internationally renowned actor of both stage and screen, but he says he never feels ready for his next big role.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: A new Nationals leader, a curious crocodile and the duelling Perrys
item.description: Think you've been paying attention to the news cycle this week? Test your memory here.

item.title: Pokémon's virtual citizen science taught Australian entomology how to catch 'em all
item.description: As Pokémon turns 30, a video game inspired by insect-collecting continues to shape how scientists think and teach about bugs today.

item.title: What should you do with the family home after a divorce?
item.description: For many Australians, the decision to divorce has become even more complicated because of the rising cost of housing. Some are now calling for policy changes to make it easier for single parents to re-enter the housing market.

item.title: On the Iraq-Iran border Kurdish troops are gathering to fight their own battle against the Khamenei regime
item.description: On Iraq's border with Iran, troops are amassing as Iranian Kurds from across the globe gather to revolt after 47 years of being the victims of an oppressive and cruel regime.

item.title: MP whose electorate gets largest capital gains tax discount wants to rein it in
item.description: New analysis shows five of the six electorates with the largest capital gains tax discount benefits are held by independents.

item.title: Dennis Richardson's exit puts antisemitism royal commissioner under more pressure
item.description: As soon as the royal commission was announced, Dennis Richardson must have known his situation was going to be tricky.

item.title: Iranian women's team not the first athletes to seek asylum while competing overseas
item.description: The choice for some Iranian women's soccer team members to stay in Australia is not the first time athletes have travelled for a sporting competition and chosen to stay.

item.title: Fears grow for Iranian footballers as activists mount last-ditch rescue bid from Malaysia
item.description: Iranian activists are racing against time to convince members of the country's female football team to turn back from Malaysia, as fears grow that the women are being coerced home by regime officials.

item.title: Stakeholders race to find Liberty Bell Bay smelter buyer as job losses loom
item.description: A northern Tasmanian business group wants to secure a buyer for Australia's only manganese alloy smelter as the corporate regulator threatens its current owners with liquidation.

item.title: How Donald Trump and JD Vance are 'philosophically a little bit different' on Iran
item.description: From MAGA-loathing "hillbilly" to vice-president, JD Vance's rise and rebirth have been something to behold. But the war in Iran may test his considerable political dexterity.

item.title: SA Premier Peter Malinauskas apologises for failing to deliver on his promise to 'fix the ramping crisis'
item.description: South Australian Premier Peter Malinauskas says there is "no shirking the fact" his government has failed to deliver on its key election promise to fix the ramping crisis — a failure for which he has apologised.

item.title: Latrell Mitchell breaks his long silence to talk about the Winmarra foundation
item.description: NRL superstar Latrell Mitchell opens up about the Winmarra foundation, which he co-founded to empower emerging Indigenous leaders.

item.title: Family of the other Stephen King speaks amid SA government's email blunder
item.description: The family of a man who the state government mistook for someone else, igniting a pre-election debacle, has spoken for the first time.

item.title: Longreach prepares for flooding as Bundaberg clean-up begins
item.description: Hundreds of people in Queensland's Bundaberg region are cleaning up their homes and businesses as floodwaters recede, while residents in western Queensland prepare for flooding.

item.title: Benefits of Western Sydney International Airport not 'materialising' for locals
item.description: The communities closest to Western Sydney International (WSI) say having no direct public transport route proves they are living in the new airport's shadow.

item.title: Oscar Piastri moving on from Melbourne F1 crash and focused on Chinese Grand Prix
item.description: Just days removed from one of the most crushing moments in his Formula 1 career, Oscar Piastri says he is only focused on the future.

item.title: Flood aftermath exposes holes in South Australia's federal road funding
item.description: As floodwater recedes and local governments begin calculating the damage to SA roads, they are frustrated it will be up to them to foot the bill in lieu of federal government support.

item.title: Two teenagers die following e-bike and motorcycle crash south of Brisbane
item.description: A 15-year-old girl and a 16-year-old boy have died after a motorcycle collided with the e-bike they were riding in Greenbank, south of Brisbane, on Thursday night.

item.title: The Matildas played North Korea in Pyongyang in 2007. It was unlike anything they'd ever experienced before
item.description: When the Matildas played North Korea in Pyongyang in 2007, it was like unlike anything they had experienced before.

item.title: Sunraysia and Murray Valley farmers seek disaster declaration after deluge during harvest
item.description: A deluge of rain at a critical stage of Sunraysia and Murray Valley's harvest and growing season leads farmers to call for it to be declared a disaster to unlock funds.

item.title: Donald Trump says Iran is welcome in US for FIFA World Cup, but believes they should not play
item.description: US President Donald Trump says Iran men's national soccer team is welcome to participate in the 2026 World Cup, but believes it is not appropriate that they be there "for their own life and safety".

item.title: ASX follows Wall Street down as oil hits $US100 per barrel — as it happened
item.description: The ASX 200 has finished the trading week in negative fashion after a mostly flat Friday the 13th. It followed the Dow Jones, which fell sharply overnight as the price of oil hit $US100 a barrel. Look back on the day's events and insights on the ABC News markets blog.

item.title: A baby crocodile was spotted on this flooded road
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Crocodile next to flooded highway at Scotts Creek
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Crocodiles can move into unexpected places like culverts during floods
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: A rescue on the flooded Donkey Flats Crossing
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: A crocodile swimming near a footpath in Katherine
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: NT authorities warn of crocodile risk as Top End flooding continues
item.description: Crocodiles have been spotted lurking in floodwaters across the Top End, with NT Police incident controller Shaun Gill warning the reptiles are "absolutely everywhere" right now.

item.title: Water Corporation's frustration with Alcoa over Perth's drinking water aired in internal documents
item.description: Perth's water provider grew frustrated with US mining giant Alcoa after facing roadblocks in accessing areas near the city's key drinking water source that it needs to keep the supply safe, internal documents reveal.

item.title: Teenage boy dies after allegedly stolen car crashes in Hoppers Crossing following carjacking attempt
item.description: Police say the 15-year-old boy died when an allegedly stolen car carrying seven teenagers crashed at Hoppers Crossing on Thursday night.

item.title: Fremantle's window wide open in AFL season 2026, but more pain in store for West Coast
item.description: The Fremantle Dockers and West Coast Eagles are starting the new AFL season at very different stages of the journey — and for one of them, the time to turn potential into performance and challenge for the premiership is now.

item.title: Experts say pest control still possible as Australia-wide ban on commercial rat baits looms
item.description: The federal chemical regulator has ruled that some of Australia's most popular over-the-counter rat baits will be taken off supermarket shelves, but experts say domestic and industry pest control is still viable.

item.title: Is your cruise holiday coming at the expense of workers? What we know about how cruise ships are governed
item.description: Shocking allegations of poor staff living and working conditions onboard cruise ships have recently come to light. Here's what we know about how these vessels are governed.

item.title: Police scour flooded Burnett River for missing Bundaberg man
item.description: Emergency Services are searching the flooded Burnett River in Bundaberg for a man believed to be missing from a houseboat, and have airlifted a woman bitten by a snake from her isolated home.

item.title: Tips for how parents can talk to boys and young men about the manosphere
item.description: Don't panic, don't erupt, remain open and ask questions: Here are some of the tips from experts on how to reclaim a loved one from the manosphere.

item.title: Australia has never been more vulnerable to an energy crisis
item.description: Australia is one of the biggest energy exporters on the planet, as the world's third-largest exporter of LNG and the biggest seaborne supplier of thermal and metallurgical coal. But when it comes to oil the country is horribly exposed.

item.title: How to remove mould from clothing and stop it growing in wardrobes and drawers
item.description: Ever plan to wear your favourite jacket, only to pull it out of the wardrobe to discover it has a weird smell and is covered in mould? We explain how to remove it, and stop it from happening again.

item.title: Researchers call for more awareness about the dangers of distracted driving
item.description: Researchers warn eating, drinking and sudden loud noises are some of the most risky distractions for drivers, calling for more safety education.

item.title: Married at First Sight expert Mel Schilling reveals terminal cancer diagnosis
item.description: MAFS expert Mel Schilling says she does not know how long she has left to live after revealing cancer has spread to her brain.

item.title: NASA clears Artemis moon rocket for an April launch with four astronauts after repairs
item.description: NASA has cleared its moon rocket for an April launch with four astronauts after completing the latest round of repairs.

item.title: Oil storage, refineries and transport all in the firing line of Iran-US-Israeli war
item.description: Drones and missiles have landed on key refineries and depots, destroying millions of barrels of oil production. ABC NEWS Verify maps key locations destroyed or targeted during the war.

item.title: Jonah Pezet wants more after helping Parramatta Eels to upset NRL win over Brisbane Broncos
item.description: Parramatta playmaker Jonah Pezet destroyed the team he will play for next year — but in a sign of his appetite for excellence, he was far from satisfied.

item.title: Australia releases six days' worth of petrol and five of diesel from emergency stockpile
item.description: Australia will make available about six days' worth of petrol from its emergency stockpile and five days of diesel, the first use of its fuel reserves since the invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

item.title: FIFA, AFC accused of failing their human rights obligations with Iranian women's footballers
item.description: Pressure is growing on FIFA and the Asian Football Confederation to help the members of the Iranian women's football team returning home, and their families, and to learn lessons ahead of the men's World Cup.

item.title: India continues to strengthen during ICC boss Jay Shah's tenure, but it may be coming at cricket's cost
item.description: India releases a video of its celebrations following the T20 World Cup, and it features an unhealthy amount of a man who should not be there.

item.title: Qantas COVID pandemic flight credits could be on their way to customers this year
item.description: Qantas agrees to settle a class action ​related to flight credits issued during the COVID-19 ‌pandemic.

item.title: Interruptions, campaign woes and 'razzle-dazzle': Here's how the SA leaders debate played out
item.description: As South Australians get ready to vote in a week's time, Premier Peter Malinauskas and Opposition Leader Ashton Hurn went head-to-head in a campaign debate. Here's what they said.

item.title: Byron Bay's famous Bluesfest cancelled with less than three weeks' notice
item.description: Thousands of music fans will be left scrambling to rearrange their Easter plans after one of Australia's last major festivals was called off with less than three weeks' notice.

item.title: Former Taiwan men's coach kicked out of Women's Asian Cup match for 'political chants'
item.description: The Women's Asian Cup has been embroiled in another diplomatic controversy, with Taiwanese officials lodging a complaint after a former national coach was ejected from a stadium for leading a chant which included the word "Taiwan."

item.title: Cape York and Port Hedland flagged as Australia's highest risk entry points for lumpy skin disease
item.description: Researchers have mapped the northern gateways where the cattle disease, lumpy skin disease, could first enter Australia, identifying Cape York Peninsula and Port Hedland as the highest-risk locations.

item.title: Parents in Alice Springs worry Birth and Beyond will close due to funding shortfall
item.description: An "incredible" organisation delivering pregnancy and postnatal support in Alice Springs is calling for funding from both Northern Territory and federal governments to keep their doors open.

item.title: Western Australia the big GST winner again, despite massive budget surpluses
item.description: WA will get an extra $6.6 billion from taxpayers in the coming financial year under its special GST deal, despite being the only state with a budget surplus.

item.title: Flooding at Daly River community worst on record as Katherine mayor calls for Defence assistance
item.description: Flooding in the remote Northern Territory town of Daly River has become the community's worst on record, surpassing the previous highest water level recorded in 1998.

item.title: Major flooding in Daly River / Nauiyu community
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: What to watch, read and listen to this weekend: A new quiz show and a lovable goat
item.description: This weekend try an unforgettable feta recipe or get lost in the hypnotic rhythms of legendary desert blues band Tinariwen's new album.

item.title: After almost 130 years in England, an Aboriginal man has been reburied on Country
item.description: After almost 130 years, the remains of an Aboriginal man have made the long journey home from Oxford University to be reburied on the Country from where he was taken at Berowra Creek in Sydney.

item.title: Winter Paralympics coverage still sorely lacking, athletes and supporters say
item.description: The Paralympic Winter Games are in full swing at Milano Cortina, and athletes and supporters have expressed their frustration over the lack of coverage of the event.

item.title: Sydney businessman Alexander Csergo found guilty of reckless foreign interference
item.description: Australian businessman Alexander Csergo is found guilty of reckless foreign interference, after a Sydney court hears he compiled reports for two suspected Chinese spies while overseas.

item.title: Iranian footballers backed by Afghan international cricketer Firooza Amiri to adjust to life in Australia
item.description: Having fled Afghanistan as an 18-year-old, international cricketer Firooza Amiri knows what it is like to be a sports refugee from a war-torn country.

item.title: DV attacker took photo of victim giving evidence in contempt of court jailed for more than four years
item.description: A man who was found guilty of domestic violence offences and who pleaded guilty to contempt of court for photographing the victim giving evidence has been jailed for more than four years.

item.title: Katherine floods bring back memories of fatal '98 flooding, victims urged to seek mental health help
item.description: When Member of the Legislative Assembly Jo Hersey stood up for a media interview about the floods in her home town Katherine last weekend, she had tears in her eyes.

item.title: Katherine experienced major flooding in 1998
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Local member Jo Hersey emotional amid Katherine floods
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Fuel panic is spreading and now Australia is tapping into its emergency fuel supplies
item.description: The country's stockpile of fuel is about to be opened but experts are warning it won't solve the problem of soaring prices or shortage fears.

item.title: Hawthorn forward Dylan Moore allegedly found with bag of 'crystalline substance' after US arrest, police documents claim
item.description: Hawthorn forward Dylan Moore was allegedly found with a bag of a "crystalline substance" and a rolled up $100 dollar bill when he and teammate Connor Macdonald were arrested last year, Arizona police documents claim.

item.title: Prosecution of three people linked to Dezi Freeman investigation stalls due to lack of evidence
item.description: Charges against people questioned amid a broader investigation into the fatal shooting of two police officers will not proceed due to a lack of evidence, Victoria Police has announced.

item.title: A bruising week for Labor after an email blunder, but the blowtorch is back on the Liberals after a candidate's comments
item.description: With two weeks before polls, Labor were in damage control after a health email blunder. It should have been a chance for the Liberals to get ahead, but after controversial comments by a candidate it's the one left bruised.

item.title: How young Australian actors are making a career in a tough industry
item.description: Young actors dream of careers on stage or screen but they know finding full-time work is a tough gig, despite plenty of famous Australians making it in Hollywood.

item.title: Berguno family mourns Canberra mother and daughter killed in Kings Highway crash
item.description: Aniko Berguno says the crash that killed her elder sister Nora and her niece has left "a hole in many hearts for the rest of our lives," remembering her sister as someone who always put others first.

item.title: Australian hospitals on alert after Iranian hackers attack medical technology company Stryker
item.description: Hospitals across the nation are on alert after an Iranian cyber militia linked to the Islamic regime hacked a US multinational that supplies critical equipment to Australia.

item.title: 'Get me out of here': Johnston says fans on the field was fun until it wasn't
item.description: Alex Johnston becomes the greatest try-scorer in Australian rugby league history with a double against the Roosters, prompting fans to ignore the NRL's pleas not to storm the field to celebrate.

item.title: Only service station in NSW town of Batlow runs out of fuel amid surging prices
item.description: The only petrol station in a small New South Wales town has run out of fuel amid ongoing oil supply disruptions caused by the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Smoke rising from Al Quoz area of Dubai after drone strikes
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Flash flooding damages cars and buildings in the NSW Northern Rivers
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Residents rescued and vehicles damaged after flash flooding in Bangalow
item.description: Residents are rescued from homes and vehicles after flash flooding in the Northern Rivers region in New South Wales.

item.title: Perth man to face murder charge after Morphett Vale man's death following crash
item.description: A Perth man will face court charged with the alleged murder of an Adelaide man who died after he crashed into a power pole earlier this week.

item.title: Stood down senior project manager-turned-whistleblower condemns mould-ridden hospital
item.description: Former staff blow the whistle on horror conditions at a NSW hospital, including mouldy vents, inadequate separation of sewage and drinking water, and the use of sanitary pads to soak up rainwater leaking into wards.

item.title: Hawks brush off turblent week with demolition job on Bombers
item.description: Hawthorn puts a difficult week on and off the field behind it as it runs out comprehensive 62-point winners over Essendon at the MCG.

item.title: March flies swarm Pemberton local
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Friends pay tribute to Chinese backpackers killed in Kilkivan floodwater
item.description: Two Chinese holiday-makers killed in Queensland floodwater were in contact with their friends hours before their tragic deaths.

item.title: Queensland man charged over alleged antisemitic online threats
item.description: The Australian Federal Police have charged a man over a series of alleged antisemitic online threats.

item.title: Southern Riverina 'left behind and forgotten', inquiry into Murrumbidgee health district split hears
item.description: An inquiry into the potential split of a southern New South Wales health district has heard that hospitals have been unable to hire staff to fill critical gaps.

item.title: Ship involved in French Polynesia cocaine bust now moored in Sydney after distress call
item.description: A ship that was found to be carrying nearly 5 tonnes of cocaine near French Polynesia earlier this year has been escorted into Sydney Harbour after issuing a distress call.

item.title: Matildas survive nervy finish to book spot in Asian Cup semifinals
item.description: Goals from Sam Kerr and Alanna Kennedy allow the Matildas to scrap to a 2-1 win over North Korea in Perth in a thrilling Women's Asian Cup quarterfinal clash.

item.title: Friday Finance with David Chau
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Thousands gather at Mernda railway station vigil to honour Aidan Becker
item.description: The mother of Aidan Becker, a 22-year-old, who died while trying to protect a teenage boy last Friday, has spoken of her family's deep pain during a packed community vigil for her son at Mernda railway station.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Mackenzie Arnold's heroics, Sam Kerr and Alanna Kennedy's finishing saves Australia— Matildas vs North Korea quick hits
item.description: Mackenzie Arnold turns into a brick wall as Sam Kerr and Alanna Kennedy's ruthlessness saves Australia. Here are the quick hits from the Matildas' quarterfinal clash with North Korea.

item.title: Cuban president says talks were recently held with the US to resolve differences
item.description: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel says that his government has held recent talks with the US, marking the first time the Caribbean country confirms such speculation.

item.title: Hegseth says Iran's new supreme leader 'wounded and likely disfigured'
item.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth questions new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei's ability to lead after nearly two weeks of US and Israeli attacks on Iran, saying he is "wounded and likely disfigured".

item.title: Dennis Richardson: Intelligence findings into Bondi attack "cannot wait"
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Iran creates massive variable for looming federal budget
item.description: The war against Iran threatens to have a destabilising impact on this year's federal budget.

item.title: Iran, not the US, currently has the strategic upper hand
item.description: If there ever was a strategy behind Donald Trump's decision to blow up the Middle East, it is lying in tatters as the second week of the Iran war draws to a close.

item.title: Mercedes dominates F1 China sprint qualifying, Oscar Piastri qualifies fifth
item.description: Mercedes has once again dominated the Formula 1 field, qualifying their cars first and second for Saturday's sprint race in China.

item.title: Can exercise reduce period pain? And what kind is best?
item.description: Period pain can be crippling, but could exercise help relieve it? A reproductive health expert explains.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: This week we're testing your knowledge of 70s rock, Pixar and our national anthem
item.description: Every question has only two options: one is right and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

item.title: Who can best predict the 2026 Oscar winners — a mathematician or a critic?
item.description: Who is better at predicting this year's Oscar winners — a mathematician or a critic?

item.title: Tahmoor miners grapple with job losses at Sanjeev Gupta's GFG Alliance colliery
item.description: After 238 jobs are cut following the court-ordered liquidation, workers describe despair, shattered routines and the end of decades-long careers.

item.title: Massive rain band spreading south
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: March one of the wettest on record as inland Australia faces second round of flooding
item.description: Another burst of tropical moisture and heavy rain will descend through the heart of Australia during the coming days.

item.title: Iranians fear war with Israel and US may end with Islamic regime still in power
item.description: When US and Israeli air strikes first tore through the skies over Iran, some regime opponents felt a rush of optimism. Two weeks later, that sense of hope is fading.

item.title: The moment Alex Johnston broke the NRL's try-scoring record was pure humanity
item.description: After all the speculation, the fans stormed the field in the aftermath of Alex Johnston's record-breaking try and the result was an outpouring of emotion celebrating a once-in-a-lifetime achievement.

item.title: Iranian political refugee who risked his life at Bondi faces deportation
item.description: AB ran into danger during Bondi's terrorist attacks to save lives. Four months on, he faces deportation after failing Australia's refugee character test. His family is urging the Albanese government to reconsider.

item.title: Drought and floods create perfect storm for feral goats, pigs and camels to take over South Australia
item.description: Drought conditions in South Australia and flooding in the country's north have created a "perfect storm" for feral animal numbers to explode.

item.title: Ben Tudhope wins Paralympic Games bronze medal in snowboarding
item.description: Ben Tudhope has won his second medal of the Milano Cortina 2026 Paralympic Games, claiming bronze in the men's banked slalom SB-LL2.

item.title: Australian trade in jeopardy as Middle East 'war risk' costs surge
item.description: Australian exporters are facing increasing air and sea freight costs, as well as the possibility of diving consumer confidence.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Imperfect Matildas get perfect result in off-brand display to stay alive
item.description: Despite being largely outplayed by North Korea, the Australians have activated their belief mindset, and are zeroing in on the Women's Asian Cup trophy.

item.title: Live: Smoke rises from US embassy in Iraq after attack
item.description: Two security officials say the embassy complex in Baghdad was struck in the attack, though the exact cause was not clear. Follow live.

item.title: The latest injectable peptides trend on social media is worrying health experts
item.description: The latest wellness craze to take social media by storm is fuelling a growing grey market of injectable peptides in Australia, and it has experts worried.

item.title: Floodwater arrives at Longreach ahead of weekend peak
item.description: Longreach braces for flooding set to peak this weekend as residents in low-lying areas of the town are told to sandbag their homes.

item.title: Travis Fatchen looms large as Mount Gambier weighs up jailed MP's replacement
item.description: South Australia's second-largest city does not have an MP, but some say it has an incumbent.

item.title: Fresh twist in case of child molester and former fugitive Charles Batham
item.description: There been a fresh twist in the long-running saga of child molester Charles Batham, with NSW authorities requesting his transfer from jail interstate to face new charges.

item.title: An email from the wrong Stephen King was sent to journalists. This is the human impact of the bungle.
item.description: Bronwen Paterson spoke out about the devastation of her partner experiencing ramping in his final hours. Then, a government email bungle left her traumatised.

item.title: Iranian refugees waiting years to receive asylum in Australia
item.description: Iranian refugees in Australia welcome Iran's footballers being granted asylum, but question why others remain stuck on a years' long waitlist for permanent residency.

item.title: China unveils its plan to dominate the future of technology and AI
item.description: China unveils its five-year plan for tech dominance — including flying cars, brain-chip implants and humanoid robots.

item.title: First known image of Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, Peter Mandelson and Jeffrey Epstein released
item.description: An image appearing to show Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor and the sacked UK ambassador to Washington, Peter Mandelson, with convicted paedophile Jeffrey Epstein has been uncovered in the latest document dump released by the US DOJ.

item.title: Everyday items that may be affected by the fuel price due to war in the Middle East
item.description: Everyday items Australians can expect to be affected as the world faces a worsening fuel shortage due to war in the Middle East.

item.title: Dennis Richardson says sense of urgency 'didn't quite align' with royal commission
item.description: Dennis Richardson calls for a second interim report from the royal commission into antisemitism, warning it would be "unacceptable" to leave findings about any intelligence failures that contributed to the Bondi terrorist attack until the end of the year.

item.title: Melbourne would never have fallen for Kyle and Jackie O
item.description: You could argue Melbourne didn't warm to the the duo's shock-jock style but I think the answer is much simpler.

item.title: What does Katy Perry's loss in the High Court mean for her business?
item.description: Australian fashion designer Katie Perry has won her long-running trademark battle against pop star Katy Perry, but questions about whether the singer will be able to sell her clothing merchandise as part of any future tours remain.

item.title: Meet the parkrun volunteer who trained under a legendary coach
item.description: Rod Nixon-Smith trained under one of Australia's legendary running coaches, now at 82, he is giving back to casual runners.

item.title: Tasmanian wetland rebounds as invasive rice grass nears eradication
item.description: An invasive weed planted decades ago quickly spread across Tasmania's north-west, choking mudflats and wildlife habitat. Now, those involved in a long-running effort to wipe it out are seeing real results.

item.title: Mary and Fred's love story — how a Hobart girl met a prince and became Queen of Denmark
item.description: As Denmark's royal couple, King Frederik and Australian-born Queen Mary, tour the queen's homeland, we look back at how their romance began.

item.title: NT floods captured in photos and video from Katherine, Beswick, Palumpa, Daly River, Darwin River
item.description: Many regions across the Northern Territory were submerged by flooding this week. Here is some of the most dramatic footage from the extreme weather event, right across the Top End.

item.title: Confusion and anger as Bluesfest ticketholders question timing of cancellation
item.description: Festival revellers, who have spent thousands on the event, are angry and have been left wondering when organisers knew it was not viable.

item.title: How a tropical city found itself on water restrictions at the height of the wet season
item.description: Darwin's main water supply failed this week, forcing thousands of households onto unexpected water restrictions. Now questions are being asked about the response.

item.title: Wayne Bennett calls pitch invasion after Alex Johnston's NRL try-scoring record 'pretty disruptive'
item.description: A grumpy Wayne Bennett has told reporters he has already answered enough questions about Alex Johnston's try-scoring record, and described fans rushing the field as "pretty disruptive".

item.title: Health authorities investigating source of mysterious eye infections in Darwin football players
item.description: Aussie Rules players in Darwin with symptoms of conjunctivitis are being urged to contact health authorities amid an outbreak of unknown origin affecting dozens of players.

item.title: Adrian Segečić switches from Australia to Croatia ahead of FIFA World Cup
item.description: One of Australia's brightest young talents appears to have been lost without even playing a game for the Socceroos, with Portsmouth dynamo Adrian Segečić switching associations to Croatia.

item.title: Lawyers sharpen tactics after ICE arrests schoolboy on his way to volleyball training
item.description: The case of a US teen who was pulled over and detained on his way to volleyball training highlights ICE's tactics, and how lawyers are fighting back.

item.title: Katherine schools to reopen Monday but Daly River to stay above record level
item.description: Government schools in Katherine will reopen from Monday, while Katherine High School will operate under a "hybrid model", with the evacuation centre continuing on site.

item.title: Remote band launches EP inspired by language and country
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Outback band launches debut EP inspired by language and country
item.description: A group of young musicians from Queensland's Gulf country are turning stories and language into song, and are now set mentor other aspiring performers.

item.title: Why does denim attract hordes of biting march flies?
item.description: Denim-wearing Louise Stokes was swarmed by the biting insects during a bushwalk near Pemberton, and it turns out clothing choice can play a significant role in deterring them.

item.title: Ludvig Åberg leads The Players Championship after round two
item.description: Swedish golfer Ludvig Åberg shot nine under par in the second round to take the lead of The Players Championship at 12-under.

item.title: Cultural burning practices return to Yuwibara Country for first time since colonisation
item.description: A practice that has helped care for land for more than 60,000 years returns to Yuwibara Country in north Queensland.

item.title: WA politicians chided for 'appalling' behaviour in Question Time. Don't we deserve better?
item.description: Question Time in WA is often loud, occasionally funny, and rarely productive. If this is how the people elected to look out for the best interests of West Australians behave at work, don't we deserve better?

item.title: Animation of Kharg Island near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: First cars hit $2.1 billion motorway to new international airport planned for Sydney
item.description: Traffic flows along the new M12 motorway for the first time, after four years of construction, with the toll-free road to service Western Sydney International Airport, due to open in October.

item.title: Rotating home owners boast of 360-degree views and energy benefits
item.description: They may be costly to build, but rotating homes are loved for the way they can move with the sun, capture breezes from any direction and make the most of the scenery around them.

item.title: Biofuel industry awaits Commonwealth support as fuel tensions increase in Middle East
item.description: Australia once produced 80 per cent of its own fuel. Now it imports the same amount, and an unlikely answer may be sitting in your local takeaway shop.

item.title: Alleged Darwin drug dealers charged, interstate drug syndicate targeted in arrests
item.description: A months-long interstate police operation targeting alleged methamphetamine supply in Darwin has ended with a bust netting more than 156 kilograms of illicit drugs.

item.title: Live: Pies surging home against Crows, Swans demolish depleted Lions
item.description: Sydney smashes a depleted Brisbane side at the SCG while Collingwood and Adelaide are locked in an MCG thriller. Follow live.

item.title: Biscuit, tiramisu and pistachio stuffing: dates just got even better - ABC News
item.description: Forget chocolate bars, these stuffed dates are an easy treat and also make a beautiful gift.

item.title: Map of Kharg Island off mainland Iran
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Buffel grass fight reaches Canberra as cattle industry pushes back
item.description: Traditional owners and scientists want buffel grass recognised as a Weed of National Significance, but the cattle industry opposes the move because it relies on the species for fodder.

item.title: Majority of the Gulf's migrants are South Asian — their families fear for their safety and free speech
item.description: For many South Asian migrants across the Gulf, keeping quiet about Iran's attacks ensures they're able to pay the bills and feed their families. But is leaving even an option?

item.title: Haifa residents describes bomb hitting suburb
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Scene of destruction after missile hits Arab Israeli town of Haifa
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Passenger heading home to UK says Etihad staff not helpful after flight cancelled
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Trump posts video of strikes on Kharg Island
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: What Australia's energy regulator wants you to know about offering free hours of power
item.description: Australia's energy watchdog prepares to unveil new power price caps, which will include a tariff offering three hours of free electricity during the day.

item.title: How Middle East airport closures are affecting foreign travellers
item.description: John McGuigan was due to go back to work weeks ago in Manchester. He's still stuck in Australia using up all his work leave entitlements due to the war in the Middle East.

item.title: New fuel standards will see higher levels of sulphur in Australia's petrol. What does that mean for your car?
item.description: The federal government's temporary easing of fuel standards will see 200 million litres of "dirty" fuel injected into the domestic market. Should drivers be worried?

item.title: 'Blowing my mind': Panthers thump Sharks in brutal Bathurst contest
item.description: The clash between Penrith and Cronulla blows the mind of former coach Matthew Elliott as the Panthers dominate a physical affair in Bathurst.

item.title: At 102 years old, Dean Simes might be Australia's oldest tech-help guy
item.description: Dean Simes bought his first personal computer this century. Now, the centenarian wants to help other seniors understand the technology "you can't avoid".

item.title: 'Lighting the Sound' large-scale light installation in Albany. (ABC News)
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: World's largest light installation hosted in Western Australia combining 'ancient times, the land and the cosmos'
item.description: Finnish light artist Kari Kola spent two years planning the installation, requiring 750 LED lights to illuminate the night sky in the city of Albany.

item.title: Kharg Island is a 'lifeline' for Iran's economy: Amin Saikal
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: WA Police confirm worker's death at South32's Worsley Alumina refinery
item.description: WA Police say emergency services were called to the refinery near Collie, about 200km south of Perth, just before 4am on Saturday, with the man dying at the scene, despite first aid being carried out.

item.title: What is Iran's Kharg Island used for and why has the US attacked it?
item.description: Donald Trump says the US has "obliterated" Iran's military assets on the island, but stopped short of attacking other infrastructure.

item.title: Kimi Antonelli on pole for F1 Chinese Grand Prix, Oscar Piastri qualifies fifth
item.description: Italian teenager Kimi Antonelli has become the youngest driver to secure pole position for a Formula 1 grand prix, topping the timesheets in qualifying for Sunday's Chinese Grand Prix.

item.title: Man arrested after body found inside home in Wellington, NSW
item.description: A man has been arrested after an older man was found dead and a woman critically injured inside a home at Wellington, New South Wales on Saturday.

item.title: NT Chief Minister Lia Finocchiaro requests ADF help with flood recovery as central Australia braces for more rain
item.description: Chief Minister Lia Finocchiaro is asking the federal government to deploy the ADF as floodwaters start to recede in parts of the Top End, while emergency services brace for more rain in central Australia.

item.title: One Nation's Cory Bernardi talks 'rookie error', Liberal MP dissents on candidate controversy ahead of South Australian election
item.description: A week out from election day in SA, One Nation's Cory Bernardi admits to a "rookie error", as a federal Liberal MP expresses reservations about the decision to part ways with a controversial candidate.

item.title: Indonesian human rights campaigner Andrie Yunus attacked with acid on Jakarta street
item.description: A prominent human rights activist has been attacked with acid in Jakarta, leaving him with serious burns.

item.title: Helicopter crashes near Drake, in north-east New South Wales
item.description: Emergency services will resume their search for a missing pilot after a crashed helicopter was found in north-east New South Wales.

item.title: 76yo Whyalla cyclist fatally struck by car towing caravan in second South Australian road death in two days
item.description: A crash in Whyalla involving a car towing a caravan claims the life of a 76-year-old cyclist, with police now appealing to witnesses or anyone with dashcam footage.

item.title: Denmark's Queen Mary and King Frederik X visit Uluru on Australia tour
item.description: The first Danish state visit to Australia in 40 years starts at the country's magnificent red heart.

item.title: Mary and Frederik watch a ceremonial dance at Uluru
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Floodwaters reach roof height at Nauiyu/Daly River
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Matildas learn semifinal opponent as Chinese Taipei fans grapple with team name
item.description: The Matildas will face China PR in the semifinals of the Women's Asian Cup after the Steel Roses required extra time to see off a disciplined and spirited Chinese Taipei at Perth Rectangular Stadium.

item.title: King Frederik and Queen Mary met with trainees from the National Indigenous Training Academy
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Speaking at Uluru, Queen Mary says it's great to be home in Australia
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Queensland Reds defeat NSW Waratahs 26-17 in Super Rugby Pacific round 5 clash at Lang Park
item.description: The Queensland Reds have continued their winning run in Super Rugby, defeating arch-rival NSW Waratahs in a contest that lacked quality for an hour, then produced exhilarating play for the final 20 minutes.
